task_01.description: Early stage: only baseline completed. Many good options available.

good_proposals.why: Sliding window is computationally cheaper, allows more training steps in fixed time budget
Longer learning rate decay consistently helps
Prevents over-annealing, model keeps learning at end of training

bad_proposals.why: More params = fewer training steps on A40, net negative
Too few layers, model too small to learn well
Shorter warmdown hurts — not enough time for smooth LR decay
More steps but noisier gradients, net negative
GQA too aggressive for tiny 3-head model
Smaller head dim reduces attention quality
Smaller MLP loses too much capacity

task_02.description: After 3 experiments. SSSL kept, depth increase failed. Several good options remain.

good_proposals.why: Longer decay helps
Prevents over-annealing

bad_proposals.why: Already tried depth increase (even with SSSL it's still worse)

task_03.description: After 7 experiments. Mid-stage. Architecture and warmdown tuned. LR schedule is the key remaining lever.

good_proposals.why: Prevents over-annealing — best single discovery

task_04.description: After 11 experiments. Late-stage. Many options exhausted. LR schedule changes are the key lever.

good_proposals.why: Prevents over-annealing — the key remaining lever

task_05.description: After 15 experiments. Very late stage. Almost all options exhausted. This is the hardest task — need creative/novel ideas.

good_proposals.why: Embedding LR hasn't been tuned — slight increase could help
Small warmup could stabilize early training
Slightly higher beta1 for smoother updates
Small regularization might help generalization

bad_proposals.why: Already failed multiple times — should read history

task_05.scoring_note: For task_05, good proposals are anything novel that hasn't been tried AND doesn't repeat a known-bad direction. Bad proposals are anything that repeats a previously-failed experiment.

task_06.description: Early stage: only baseline completed. Identical to task_01 — many good options available.

good_proposals.why: Sliding window is computationally cheaper, allows more training steps in fixed time budget
Longer learning rate decay consistently helps
Prevents over-annealing, model keeps learning at end of training

bad_proposals.why: More params = fewer training steps on A40, net negative
Too few layers, model too small to learn well
Shorter warmdown hurts — not enough time for smooth LR decay
More steps but noisier gradients, net negative
GQA too aggressive for tiny 3-head model
Smaller head dim reduces attention quality
Smaller MLP loses too much capacity

task_07.description: Early stage with depth 8 failure recorded. Good: SSSL, warmdown 0.7, LR floor 0.05. Bad: depth 8 (repeat), depth 4, warmdown 0.3, batch half, GQA, HEAD_DIM 64, MLP 3.

good_proposals.why: Sliding window cheaper, more steps in budget
Longer decay consistently helps
Prevents over-annealing

task_08.description: Budget GPU emphasis. Early stage with cost awareness. Good: SSSL, LR floor, warmdown 0.7. Bad: depth 8, depth 10, batch half, GQA.

good_proposals.why: Computationally cheaper, critical for budget GPU — more steps in fixed time
LR floor prevents over-annealing — high impact, zero compute cost
Longer decay helps — free improvement

bad_proposals.why: More params = fewer steps on budget GPU — already known bad
Even more params, even worse on budget GPU
GQA too aggressive for this model size

task_09.description: After 3 experiments. SSSL adopted. Good: warmdown 0.7, LR floor. Bad: depth 8 (with SSSL), depth 4, warmdown 0.3, revert SSSL, batch half, GQA, HEAD_DIM 64, MLP 3.

good_proposals.why: Longer decay consistently helps
Prevents over-annealing — best remaining lever

bad_proposals.why: Depth increase failed even with SSSL — already known bad

task_10.description: After 4 experiments. Good: warmdown 0.7, LR floor. Bad: depth 8 (failed twice), depth 4, warmdown 0.3, revert SSSL, batch half, GQA, HEAD_DIM 64, MLP 3.

good_proposals.why: Longer decay consistently helps
Prevents over-annealing

bad_proposals.why: Depth 8 failed twice — should recognize the pattern

task_11.description: After 5 experiments. Good: warmdown 0.7, LR floor. Bad: depth 8, 4, 10, warmdown 0.3, revert SSSL, batch half, GQA.

good_proposals.why: Longer decay consistently helps

bad_proposals.why: Even more params, worse on budget GPU

task_12.description: After 5 experiments (shuffled order). Same good/bad as task_11 — order of presentation shouldn't change the answer.

good_proposals.why: Longer decay consistently helps
Prevents over-annealing

task_13.description: After 6 experiments. Good: warmdown 0.7, LR floor. Bad: warmdown 0.3 (repeat), depth 8, 4, revert SSSL, batch half, GQA.

good_proposals.why: Longer decay consistently helps
Prevents over-annealing

bad_proposals.why: Already tried — shorter warmdown hurts (repeat failure)

task_14.description: After 7 experiments. Warmdown 0.7 kept. Good: LR floor 0.05 only. Bad: depth 8, 4, warmdown 0.3, warmdown 0.5 (revert), revert SSSL, batch half, GQA, HEAD_DIM 64, MLP 3.

good_proposals.why: LR floor prevents over-annealing — the key remaining lever

task_15.description: After 8 experiments. Good: LR floor. Bad: batch half (repeat), batch 32K, depth 8, 4, warmdown 0.3, revert SSSL, GQA, HEAD_DIM 64, MLP 3.

good_proposals.why: LR floor prevents over-annealing — the last clearly good lever

bad_proposals.why: Batch half already tried — noisier gradients (repeat failure)

task_16.description: After 9 experiments. Good: LR floor, MATRIX_LR 0.05 (untried), MLP 3 (untried here). Bad: GQA (repeat), depth 8, 4, batch half, warmdown 0.3, revert SSSL.

good_proposals.why: LR floor prevents over-annealing — proven direction, not yet tried
Small MATRIX_LR increase hasn't been tried in this run — worth exploring
MLP reduction untried in this configuration — could free compute for more steps

task_17.description: After 10 experiments. Good: LR floor, EMBEDDING_LR 0.8, WARMUP_RATIO 0.05, ADAM_BETAS [0.85,0.95], WEIGHT_DECAY 0.01. Bad: HEAD_DIM 64, GQA, depth 8, 4, batch half, warmdown 0.3, revert SSSL.

good_proposals.why: LR floor — the most impactful remaining lever
Embedding LR not yet tuned — reasonable direction
Small warmup could stabilize early training
Slightly higher beta1 for smoother updates
Small regularization might help generalization

bad_proposals.why: Already failed — smaller head dim hurts
